title: Instalaciones de almacenamiento en frĂ­o y congeladores - Elite Mexico
content:
Resinous flooring systems are highly preferred for cold storage and freezer environments because of their ability to perform reliably under extreme low temperatures. These seamless, non-porous coatings resist moisture, thermal shock, and microbial growthâ€”making them ideal for facilities that require strict hygiene standards. Their resilience against freezing and thawing cycles prevents cracking and delamination, while specialized low-temperature cure formulations allow for efficient installation without disrupting operations. With options for slip resistance and chemical durability, resinous floors provide a safe, long-lasting solution for these high-demand spaces.
Advantages
Thermal Shock Resistance
: Endures rapid temperature changes without cracking or failure.
Seamless & Non-Porous Surface
: Blocks moisture, bacteria, and contaminants from penetrating the floor.
Slip-Resistant Finishes
: Enhances safety in wet, icy, or foggy conditions.
Low-Temperature Curing Options
: Allows installation even in active freezer environments.
Chemical Resistance
: Withstands cleaning agents, disinfectants, and incidental chemical exposure.
Impact & Load Durability
: Handles heavy pallet jacks, forklifts, and cold storage racking systems.
Hygienic & Easy to Clean
: Supports sanitation protocols with minimal maintenance.
Long-Term Performance
: Designed for durability in constant cold, wet, and demanding conditions.
Polymer Modified Cement
Polymer Modified Cement
